content.full_text:
~

RAVI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION

August 21, 1995

Philip Bruce Raful, Treasurer
Committee to Elect Kerry Mazzoni
P.O. Box 2753
Novato, CA 94948

Re: Your Request For Advice
Our File No. A-95-241

Dear Mr. Raful:

This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

Is the sponsorship of a breakfast by Pacific Telesis at a
forum convened by Assemblymember Mazzoni a reportable in-kind
campaign contribution?

CONCLUSION

Sponsorship of the breakfast by Pacific Telesis is a
reportable in-kind contribution that must be disclosed on
Assemblymember Mazzoni's campaign statement.

FACTS

Assemblymember Kerry Mazzoni convened a business workshop in
San Rafael on March 24, 1995, as a forum on business opportunities
for general, small, disabled veteran-owned, women-owned, and
minority-owned businesses. The forum was a workshop designed to
assist business owners in Marin and Sonoma Counties. The event
was free of charge and open to the public. Attendees included
members of chambers of commerce, community organizations,

___________________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814 (916) 322-5660

Mr. Raful
Our File No. A-95-241
Page Two

contractors, and sub-contractors. No elected officials were
invited to attend the event. Pacific Telesis has notified
Assemblymember Mazzoni that sponsorship of the breakfast is going
to be reported as a $600 in-kind contribution to the committee.

ANALYSIS

A "contribution" is defined as a payment2/ "except to the
extent that full and adequate consideration is received unless it
is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made
for political purposes." The term "contribution" also includes
any transfer of anything of value received by a committee from
another committee, unless full and adequate consideration is
received. (Section 82015.) Regulation 18215 further clarifies
that a payment is made for political purposes if it is received by
or made at the behest3/ of a candidate, unless it is clear from
surrounding circumstances that the payment was received or made at
his or her behest for personal purposes unrelated to his or her
candidacy or status as an officeholder.

Typically, when an officeholder receives services or anything
else of value for which he or she has not provided full and
adequate consideration, the officeholder has received either a
gift or an in-kind "non-monetary" contribution. Because the
payment by Pacific Telesis was made at Assemblymember Mazzoni's
behest for purposes related to her status as an officeholder, it
would appear that the sponsorship of the breakfast by Pacific
Telesis is an in-kind contribution.

The fair market value of the breakfast must be reported on
Assemblymember Mazzoni's campaign statement as a non-monetary
contribution from Pacific Telesis. Generally, the fair market
value of goods or services which are non-monetary contributions is
whatever it would cost the candidate receiving the goods or
services to purchase them on the open market.

2/ "Payment" means a payment, distribution, transfer, loan,
advance, deposit, gift or other rendering of money, property,
deposit,
services or anything else of value, whether tangible or
intangible. (Section 82044.)

3/ "Made at the behest" means a payment made under the control
or at the direction of, in cooperation, consultation,
coordination, or concert with, or at the request or suggestion of
an officer, agent or member of a committee of a
candidate, controlled committee, official of a committee of a
candidate, controlled committee, official of a candidate, a
party, or organization formed or existing primarily for
political purposes. (Regulation 18215(b).)

Mr. Raful
Our File No. A-95-241
Page Three


If you have additional questions, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

[Signature: Dixie Howard]

by: Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
